# DECISIONS (ADR-lite: one row per real choice)

| Date | Task | Decision | Options considered | Why | Owner |
|------|------|----------|--------------------|-----|-------|
| 2026-07-14 | intake | MVP billing = PaymentPort adapter + stub; vendor vetted by scout at P3 | pin vendor now / cut billing from R1 / port + stub | closed beta (gate 5 pending) needs no real payments; Stripe absent in Morocco; matches ADR-05 port pattern, zero rework on vendor pick | human |
| 2026-07-14 | intake | Drizzle stands; D1-C1 "Prisma schema" read as pre-S2 placeholder | Prisma / Drizzle | S2-D1 is the later dedicated toolchain ruling with verification log; CLAUDE.md agrees; work items (schema, registry generator, delete-coverage test) carry over unchanged | human |
| 2026-07-14 | intake | PRD.md + Architecture.md accepted as the signed pack | fetch packet YAMLs first / accept / accept-with-P1-blocker | packet content verified inline (locked decisions, exports, verification log, open risks); only change_requests log absent, empty by construction | human |
| 2026-07-14 | intake | Empty states: standing rule — every UI task card specs its four states + empty copy per D4-A2 formula; reviewer enforces | upfront EMPTY-STATES.md / human-drafted copy / task-card rule | avoids front-loading copy for screens weeks out; enforcement point already exists (reviewer) | human |
| 2026-07-14 | T-001 | Base linter = ESLint 9 flat + typescript-eslint; no-explicit-any hard-error in gate/resolution | Biome / ESLint | pack routed the lint slot to S2 but never pinned a tool; ESLint is the Next 16 ecosystem default and typescript-eslint carries the pack's no-any rule directly | orchestrator |
| 2026-07-14 | T-001 | pnpm pinned at installed 11.3.0 via packageManager field | downgrade to v10 line / pin installed 11.3 | pack's "v10 line" meant current-stable-at-observation (2026-07-07); a year later 11.x is that; downgrading a working toolchain buys nothing, lockfile pins exact versions | orchestrator |
| 2026-07-14 | T-001 | i18n package may import shared only (row absent from S1-A2 edge table) | shared-only / db+shared | narrowest edge consistent with the table's spirit; widen in T-002 review only if message catalogs prove to need db | orchestrator |
| 2026-07-14 | T-001 | Reviewer finding 1 overruled: jscpd flag stays --exit-code | revert to --exitCode (reviewer, per docs) / keep --exit-code | CI runtime is authoritative: jscpd 5.0.12 binary rejected --exitCode and named --exit-code as the valid flag (run 29334709943); docs lag the 5.x CLI | orchestrator |
| 2026-07-14 | T-002 | "Provider SDK" (S1-A2 §4's agent-only import rule) expressed as an explicit named-vendor regex (openai, @anthropic-ai/, @google/generative-ai, cohere-ai, @mistralai/, groq-sdk, replicate, exa-js, firecrawl, serpapi, @tavily/, etc.) in .dependency-cruiser.cjs | wait until S2/S3 pick a vendor / block all non-workspace npm deps outside agent / named-vendor allowlist now | can't leave the rule unenforced until vendor pick lands; blocking all npm deps in every non-agent package false-positives on react/next/zod/drizzle; named list is precise now, cheap to extend when a vendor is chosen | b4 |
| 2026-07-14 | T-002 | Committed dependency graph artifact format = Mermaid text (docs/build/dependency-graph.mmd) via `depcruise --output-type mermaid` | dot/svg via Graphviz / archi json / mermaid text | mermaid needs no native binary in CI or on dev machines and GitHub renders it inline; dot/svg would add a Graphviz dependency for zero benefit at scaffold stage | b4 |
